generalNotes: This itinerary includes a Trip‑a‑Deal guided tour covering Bulgaria, North Macedonia, Albania, Montenegro, Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Slovenia.
Independent travel days occur in Sofia, Zagreb, and Budapest.
Multiple time zones are crossed - ensure devices auto‑update.
Keep passports accessible for frequent border crossings in the Balkans.
Local currencies vary - card acceptance is widespread but cash is useful in smaller towns.
weather: May in the Balkans is spring - expect mild to warm temperatures (15–26°C).
Rain showers are possible, especially in mountainous regions.
Coastal areas (Montenegro, Croatia) are warmer and sunnier.
Budapest and Zagreb are typically warm with low rainfall in late May.
transportBasics: Trip‑a‑Deal provides coach transport for guided days.
FlixBus used for Zagreb → Budapest transfer.
Local taxis and rideshare available in major cities.
Public transport reliable in Sofia, Ljubljana, Zagreb, Budapest.
Border crossings may require passport checks - keep documents accessible.

segments.title: Trip-a-Deal Day 7 - Budva Free Day
segments.description: Free day in Budva, Montenegro (Virpazar & Lake Skadar excursion).
notes: Today enjoy a day at leisure in Budva, the centre of Montenegrin tourism, known for its well-preserved medieval walled city, sandy beaches and diverse nightlife.

Pre-paid Activity: Virpazar Traditional Villages, including a boat ride to Shkoder Lake. Virpazar is a gateway to Skadar Lake National Park, offering boat tours that explore traditional villages like Karuch and the lake's unique ecosystem with a wide variety of birds and water lilies. The boat trip includes scenic routes through narrow channels, visiting historical sites such as the medieval Kom Monastery or Lesendro fortress. A boat ride, park entrance and transportation are included.

cost.costNotes: Included in Trip-a-Deal package (optional excursion extra)
